message: Jake
Garrison
Following Yuriko's suggestion, I came to the office of NETS, which is Guy's team.
It's a ridiculous situation to come to the stadium during the offseason since the team office is inside the stadium.
Upon entering, there's a reception area with two receptionists sitting.
Receptionist A
Welcome to the NETS team office.
Receptionist B
What brings you here today?
Uh
Well, even though I came, what on earth should I explain?
Receptionist A
Um, how can we help you?
Receptionist B
Are you a fan here for a visit?
Uh
Right
Honestly, I should probably just say I'm Guy's friend.
Hey, hey, what’s going on with you guys?
Receptionist A
Ah,
San.
Receptionist B
Could you please assist this customer? I really don’t understand what's happening.
While I was fumbling, it turned into a terrible situation.
I am the manager of player affairs.
What brings you to our team?
See
Talk
Listen

System

Reception
Guy's matter
About　the　NETS

The reception area of the baseball team's office has a high ceiling and lighting that's so bright it's blinding.
The space, stripped down to priority functionality, is only decorated by the two receptionists and team flags hanging behind them.
The team uniforms look good on them, but the downside is that you can hardly see their faces.
Do they sit here all day?
That guy looks like a bit of a rotund, easygoing uncle.
But beneath that cheerful smile, there could be a surprisingly shrewd persona hidden.
What can I do for you?
I’m a friend of Guy,
Snipes, to be exact. I was hoping you could give me Guy's contact information.
If you’re his friend, you must know his contact details, right?
Of course I know his home address, but he’s traveling right now.
I’d like to know how to reach him while he’s away.
I can’t quite decide if I should trust you.
I assure you, I don’t intend to cause any trouble. However, Guy is quite popular with lots of fans.
How can I prove my trustworthiness?
Could you share a bit about yourself?
I’m Snipes, a private detective operating in Manhattan.
I was at Columbia University with Guy.
That sounds familiar.
Oh, right! The friend Guy saved from drugs!
Y-yes, well.
Is that so? Wow, it seems you really turned your life around!
I’m busy even though it’s the off-season.
Well, actually, I feel a bit awkward saying this, but...
We don’t know where Guy is, either.
Huh, really?
It's quite an embarrassing story.
If you get any news, could you let me know as well? I’m really stuck since it’s the time for contract renewals.
Did he really go on a trip during such an important time?
I wonder what Guy is thinking.
No, saying this to you just turns it into grumbling. Sorry about that.
Please let me know if you find out anything.
My beloved NETS is a National League baseball team with a home ground in New York.
We almost won the championship last season, right?
Yeah, just a step away from victory.
It was indeed a regrettable season.
Although its capacity is inferior to the Yankees' stadium in the American League, the environment is better, right?
Yeah, I think it’s a wonderful environment.
Yeah, that's right, that's right.
If you're living in New York, you must be a NETS fan, right?
I assumed the baseball team would at least have a way to contact Guy.
Where on earth is Guy and what is he doing?
He’s such a troublesome guy, causing trouble for everyone around.
The fact that even the team doesn’t know about Guy suggests...
He hasn’t gone missing, has he?
Even during the off-season, the team office doesn’t actually take a break.
Last season we were unfortunately the runners-up, but I hope we can win it all next season with that performance.
Please let me know if you find out anything about Guy.
Ｖ＝
Ｎ＝
